meaning: n.(人体的)重要器官,要害,命脉
examples: His vitals were tortured by this problem.
He had a citified air about him that ate into Tom's vitals.
The nurse checked Keith's vitals and temperature and wrote them down on a chart by the bed.
Body sensors, some built into shirts, are letting doctors remotely monitor and diagnose vitals.
Electronic health record vendor athenahealth partners with review site Vitals, in which it also has an investment.
